entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 27
entries.excerpt: “Isaac was old, and his eyes were dim… and he said… to Esau: Take me venison: and make me savoury meat… that I may eat, that my soul may bless thee before I die” (Gen 27:1, 3–4). Esau went hunting. Hearing this, Rebekah advises Jacob to go to his father, lest Esau receive the birthright contrary to what God had said: “the elder shall serve the younger.” But he did not listen to her, because he feared to receive a curse instead of a blessing. But when Rebekah said: “Upon me be thy curse” (Gen 27:13), then Jacob did as Rebekah commanded.…
